title: Mississippi mud pie
subtitle: Dense chocolate brownie base with marshmallows and warm ganache
excerpt: A deep, fudgy chocolate brownie baked in a pie dish, topped with marshmallows that melt into the surface, then flooded with warm dark chocolate ganache. Makes one 23 cm pie.
sourceNotes: Mississippi mud pie is a Southern American dessert documented in household baking guides from the 1970s. The name references the dark, thick mud of the Mississippi River delta, referring to the dense brownie base. The classic form combines a fudgy baked chocolate base with marshmallows and a warm ganache or fudge topping, documented in American household baking books from the late twentieth century.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best eaten warm on the day, or at room temperature the following day. The ganache firms overnight.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 190°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: A brownie or mud cake is done when the edges have set and pulled away from the sides slightly, but the centre still has a definite wobble when the tin is shaken. Unlike a custard tart, you want some fudgy underdone centre — it sets further on cooling. Insert a skewer at the edge: it should come out clean. In the centre, it should come out with a few moist crumbs, not raw batter. A fully clean skewer from the centre means the brownie has gone a little too far.

content.text: A Mississippi mud pie should be dense and fudgy all the way through — not a cake that has gone wrong, but a brownie baked in a pie dish, topped with marshmallows that melt into the surface, then flooded with warm ganache. The
is the only tricky part. Take it out while the centre still quivers.

Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} (base amount) and {{unsalted-butter}} (base amount) together in a saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat. Whisk in the {{dark-brown-sugar}}, then beat in the {{eggs}} one at a time, followed by the {{vanilla-extract}}. Sift in the {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} and fold until just combined. Fold in the {{pecans}} if using. Pour into a greased 23 cm pie dish.
Add marshmallows and bake — 25–30 minutes total

Bake for 20 minutes. Scatter the {{marshmallows}} evenly over the surface and return to the oven for 5–8 minutes, until the marshmallows are puffed and beginning to colour. The centre should still wobble. Remove and cool for 15 minutes.

Heat the {{double-cream}} in a small saucepan until steaming. Pour over the {{dark-chocolate}} (ganache amount) and leave for 30 seconds, then stir from the centre until smooth. Stir in the {{unsalted-butter}} (ganache amount). Pour over the marshmallow layer. Leave to set for 20 minutes before serving warm, or cool completely.
